reasoning:
## Analysis of Existing Solution

**Sedimentary Retirement** - Core mechanism: Automatic micro-deposits from ALL economic transactions (earning AND spending), where income volatility accelerates benefit accrual through "hardening" layers.

## Structural Approaches I Am AVOIDING:
- Transaction-based automatic capture
- Payment processor integration as primary mechanism
- Volatility as an accelerating asset
- Geological/sediment metaphors
- Capturing economic "activity" broadly

---

**Inspiration path from "unwilted":** Plants that don't wilt survive drought through invariance—their internal structure doesn't depend on consistent external water. What's the invariant in irregular work that doesn't "wilt" when income fluctuates? *Time spent working.* Hours are hours. They don't depreciate, inflate, or vary with market conditions. A gig worker's hour is as real as a CEO's hour.

```json
{
  "label": "Evergreen Hours",
  "design_principles": "Retire on TIME, not money. Work-hours are the invariant that can't wilt. Decouple retirement entirely from earnings volatility by banking the constant, not the variable.",
  "core_mechanism": "Bank verified work-hours instead of dollars. 1 hour = 1 retirement credit regardless of wage. Hours cannot depreciate or be subject to market volatility.",
  "how_it_works": "All platforms/employers report work-hours to a national ledger via API. Self-employed self-report with transaction-timestamp verification. Each hour earns one Work Hour Credit (WHC). At 65, WHCs convert to monthly payments: (Total WHCs ÷ actuarial life expectancy) × current minimum wage. Minimum-wage linkage auto-adjusts for inflation. Caregiving and education hours included via registration systems with verification. Implementation needs: universal hour-reporting standard, verification algorithms for self-reported hours, actuarial tables for conversion rates.",
  "what_is_new": "Completely decouples retirement from income levels. A $12/hour worker and $200/hour worker with identical hours get identical retirement. Banks the invariant (time) instead of the variable (pay). Not transaction-based—captures labor contribution regardless of transaction count.",
  "why_it_works": "Gig workers often work MORE hours than salaried workers—their time IS their unrecognized wealth. By banking the constant (hours) instead of the variable (pay), income volatility literally cannot affect retirement accumulation. Every hour counts equally, removing the despair of 'working hard but saving nothing.'",
  "why_it_fails": "Political resistance inevitable: high earners want their greater monetary contributions to yield greater retirement. Verification for informal work is imperfect and gameable. Creates perverse incentive toward low-productivity busywork (more hours = more credit). Ignores genuine differences in work value and difficulty. Requires massive new tracking infrastructure.",
  "medium_term": "Work platforms become retirement infrastructure, competing on hour-verification accuracy. 'Hour statements' replace 401k summaries. Workers track lifetime hours as economic citizenship metric. Debate emerges over which non-market activities qualify.",
  "long_term_vision": "Time becomes the democratic currency of retirement. A lifetime of work—at any wage—guarantees dignified old age. Economic inequality during working years stops compounding into retirement inequality. The concept of 'retirement savings' becomes archaic; you simply work your hours across a lifetime and society honors them equally. Care work, gig work, and corporate work converge into a single accounting of human effort."
}
```

solution.label: Evergreen Hours
solution.design_principles: Retire on TIME, not money. Work-hours are the invariant that can't wilt. Decouple retirement entirely from earnings volatility by banking the constant, not the variable.
solution.core_mechanism: Bank verified work-hours instead of dollars. 1 hour = 1 retirement credit regardless of wage. Hours cannot depreciate or be subject to market volatility.
solution.how_it_works: All platforms/employers report work-hours to a national ledger via API. Self-employed self-report with transaction-timestamp verification. Each hour earns one Work Hour Credit (WHC). At 65, WHCs convert to monthly payments: (Total WHCs ÷ actuarial life expectancy) × current minimum wage. Minimum-wage linkage auto-adjusts for inflation. Caregiving and education hours included via registration systems with verification. Implementation needs: universal hour-reporting standard, verification algorithms for self-reported hours, actuarial tables for conversion rates.
solution.what_is_new: Completely decouples retirement from income levels. A $12/hour worker and $200/hour worker with identical hours get identical retirement. Banks the invariant (time) instead of the variable (pay). Not transaction-based—captures labor contribution regardless of transaction count.
solution.why_it_works: Gig workers often work MORE hours than salaried workers—their time IS their unrecognized wealth. By banking the constant (hours) instead of the variable (pay), income volatility literally cannot affect retirement accumulation. Every hour counts equally, removing the despair of 'working hard but saving nothing.'
solution.why_it_fails: Political resistance inevitable: high earners want their greater monetary contributions to yield greater retirement. Verification for informal work is imperfect and gameable. Creates perverse incentive toward low-productivity busywork (more hours = more credit). Ignores genuine differences in work value and difficulty. Requires massive new tracking infrastructure.
solution.medium_term: Work platforms become retirement infrastructure, competing on hour-verification accuracy. 'Hour statements' replace 401k summaries. Workers track lifetime hours as economic citizenship metric. Debate emerges over which non-market activities qualify.
solution.long_term_vision: Time becomes the democratic currency of retirement. A lifetime of work—at any wage—guarantees dignified old age. Economic inequality during working years stops compounding into retirement inequality. The concept of 'retirement savings' becomes archaic; you simply work your hours across a lifetime and society honors them equally. Care work, gig work, and corporate work converge into a single accounting of human effort.
